items.title: Define isolated PLexus live-smoke runner boundary
items.description:
Type: runtime-boundary planning slice
Readiness: human-in-the-loop (HITL) for approval before any live smoke; documentation work is autonomous agent-ready (AFK)

Goal: define the isolated PLexus live-smoke runner boundary before DevNexus authorizes any runtime launch or gateway smoke.

Acceptance criteria:
- The proposed runner boundary names disposable runtime inputs, cleanup responsibilities, timeout behavior, process ownership, and logs/artifacts to retain.
- The boundary states which checks can run without launching live services and which require the isolated runner.
- The work item records explicit approval criteria for enabling live PLexus smoke tests in a future cycle.
- No live PLexus open/close, Docker, or host process mutation is performed in this slice.

Verification:
- Review the documented boundary against current project safety rules.
- Confirm PLexus source working tree state is preserved and no runtime process is started.

items.title: Run approved isolated PLexus live-smoke
items.description:
Type: live-runtime follow-up
Readiness: blocked on human approval and isolated runner availability
Source plan: PLAN.md priority candidate 9.

Goal: run the PLexus open/route/close live smoke only after the documented isolated runner boundary is approved and the runner harness is available.

Acceptance criteria:
- Approval names the source image or template, disposable state root, timeout budget, cleanup sequence, and retained artifacts.
- The runner uses unique workspace and target identity, copied or temporary images, and dynamic or reserved ports.
- Cleanup verifies project close, gateway route removal, process stop, copied image deletion, and artifact retention.
- No source worktree, shared image, default launcher profile, or unrelated process is mutated.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Blocked on human approval of isolated runner inputs, timeout budget, cleanup sequence, retained artifacts, and failure policy.
- Depends on the approved runner harness work in plexus:local-3 if that harness is needed before execution.

Verification:
- Execute through the approved isolated runner only.
- Record runner logs and final source worktree status.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main only for verified source changes, if any are needed.
- Live smoke logs/artifacts are retained as runtime evidence and are not themselves source publication.

items.title: Build approved isolated PLexus live-smoke runner harness
items.description:
Type: runtime harness implementation slice
Readiness: human-in-the-loop (HITL) until isolated runner inputs and cleanup policy are approved; autonomous agent-ready (AFK) for implementation after approval.
Source plan: PLAN.md priority candidate 9.

Goal: turn the documented PLexus live-smoke boundary into a reusable runner harness that can safely execute disposable open/route/close checks after approval.

Acceptance criteria:
- The runner accepts approved disposable inputs: state root, workspace id, target id, image/template source, port policy, timeout budget, cleanup sequence, and artifact retention path.
- It owns and records started processes/routes/images and verifies cleanup of only those owned resources.
- It retains logs/artifacts needed for post-run diagnosis.
- It refuses to run when approval inputs are missing or point at shared source/runtime state.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Blocked until human approval records the isolated runner inputs, timeout budget, cleanup sequence, retained artifacts, and failure policy.
- Do not launch live services, Docker, images, or host process mutation before approval.

Verification:
- Before approval, add static/unit coverage for input validation and cleanup planning only.
- After approval, run through the isolated runner and record final source worktree status.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main only for verified harness/source changes; live-run artifacts remain retained runtime evidence, not source commits.

items.title: Finish PLexus gateway lifecycle/package boundary split
items.description:
Type: package-boundary implementation slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for source refactor and unit tests; no live Pharo image or gateway process is required.
Source Vibe issues: FCD-8, FCD-10, FCD-11, FCD-13, FCD-14.

Current finding: `docs/package-boundaries.md` still says the repository is transitional: `@plexus/gateway` depends on `@plexus/core`, and lifecycle tools may still be implemented/exposed by the gateway. `packages/plexus-gateway/package.json` currently depends on `@plexus/core`.

Goal: finish the PLexus package split so the gateway is routing-only and lifecycle tools are owned by PLexus core/CLI.

Acceptance criteria:
- `@plexus/gateway` no longer imports or depends on `@plexus/core`.
- Lifecycle tools such as `plexus_project_open`, `plexus_project_close`, and `plexus_project_status` are exposed from PLexus core/owned lifecycle MCP surface instead of the routing gateway.
- Gateway-owned tools remain limited to route status, route registration/unregistration, stale-route cleanup, and forwarding calls to image MCP endpoints.
- Package metadata and docs no longer describe the split as transitional.
- Tests cover the new ownership boundary without live image startup.

Verification:
- Run package boundary/unit tests for core and gateway.
- Run `npm run typecheck --workspaces` and `npm test` when feasible.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after verification passes.

items.title: Add PLexus prepared image cache model and safe service boundary
items.description:
Type: image-cache design and implementation slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for config/model/service tests; human-in-the-loop for live image preparation or copy checks.
Source Vibe issues: FCD-2, FCD-3, FCD-4, FCD-5, FCD-6, FCD-7.

Current finding: PLexus has smoke-script image specs and startup-script MCP loading, but no durable prepared-image cache service/model. Current docs only mention a future prepared project image cache entry.

Goal: add a project-owned prepared Pharo image cache capability without pushing PLexus policy into pharo-launcher-mcp.

Acceptance criteria:
- Project config can describe a prepared image spec with configurable MCP repository, baseline/load script, cache identity, and runtime copy behavior.
- PLexus can statically validate cache specs and generate the preparation script without launching a live image.
- The cache service boundary distinguishes safe source-only operations from live prepare/copy/delete operations requiring approved runner inputs.
- Project open integration is designed so runtime images are copied from prepared cache entries before launch, but live execution remains blocked until approval.
- Unit tests cover config validation, naming, script generation, and mocked launcher calls.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Do not create, copy, launch, or delete real images until the isolated runner/live image boundary is approved.

Verification:
- Run focused PLexus config/startup-script/cache unit tests.
- Run broader typecheck/tests when feasible.

items.title: Align scoped pharo-launcher create and stop contract
items.description:
Type: scoped launcher contract implementation slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for contract/doc/unit-test alignment; human-in-the-loop for live image create/stop verification.
Source Vibe issue: FCD-75.

Current finding: `docs/kanban-agent-pharo-access.md` documents `pharo_launcher_image_create`, but `packages/plexus-core/src/scopedPharoLauncherServer.ts` exposes only list/info/start/stop, and stop currently throws `Scoped per-image stop is not implemented ... use PLexus project close policy`.

Goal: make the scoped `pharo-launcher` facade match the documented PLexus workspace policy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either implement scoped `pharo_launcher_image_create` for project-approved image specs/profiles, or remove/rename the documented create surface so docs and tools agree.
- Either implement scoped stop for workspace-owned images, or make project-close-only policy explicit in tool naming, schema, docs, and tests.
- The scoped surface cannot operate on arbitrary host-wide image names, pids, profiles, or deletion operations.
- Tests prove list/info/start/create/stop behavior uses only PLexus project/workspace state and approved image ids.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Live create/stop checks require the approved runner; unit tests should use mocks/static state.

Verification:
- Run focused scoped launcher server tests.
- Run PLexus typecheck/tests when feasible.

items.title: Broaden PLexus OS-agnostic config tests and docs
items.description:
Type: portability test/docs slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for source/docs/tests; no live runtime required.
Source Vibe issues: FCD-27, FCD-29, FCD-30.

Current finding: PLexus has some OS-aware helper tests, and docs warn that executable logic should stay OS-agnostic, but the old Vibe item still captures a useful acceptance target: generated agent/MCP config and docs should not imply Windows-only behavior.

Goal: broaden PLexus portability coverage around generated paths, config output, and documentation examples.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover Windows and POSIX-style paths for generated PLexus/agent MCP configuration where that config is produced by PLexus.
- Any remaining platform-specific behavior is behind small named helpers or clearly labeled scripts.
- Contributor and user docs distinguish portable behavior from Windows-only examples.
- No live PLexus route, image, or process startup is required.

Verification:
- Run focused config/path tests.
- Run PLexus typecheck/tests when feasible.

items.title: Expose scoped PLexus project context for DevNexus plugins
items.description:
Type: scoped runtime contract slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready (AFK) for config/model/tests; live image actions remain blocked by existing isolated-runner work.
Source plan: PLAN.md Plugin Direction and DevNexus-Pharo plugin setup requirement.

Goal: expose the PLexus scoped project/workspace/image context a DevNexus plugin such as DevNexus-Pharo can hand to subagents.

Acceptance criteria:
- PLexus has a documented/modelled scoped context keyed by project/workspace/target/image ownership rather than host-wide image names.
- The context can describe safe create/start/stop/delete affordances for owned disposable images without allowing arbitrary launcher mutation.
- Gateway routing metadata includes how a subagent obtains or records the imageId needed for routed Pharo MCP calls.
- Static/unit tests cover context validation, ownership boundaries, cleanup metadata, and route metadata without launching images.
- Existing PLexus repo/package boundaries remain intact; this is not a split into multiple Git projects.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Do not create, start, stop, delete, or mutate real images before isolated-runner approval.
- Coordinate with plexus:local-5 and plexus:local-6 rather than duplicating prepared-cache or scoped-launcher work.

Verification:
- Run focused PLexus config/context/gateway tests.
- Run npm run typecheck --workspaces and npm test when feasible.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after verification passes.

items.title: Make gateway the agent-facing Pharo MCP proxy
items.description:
Type: gateway surface implementation slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready (AFK) for docs/config/tests; no live Pharo image or gateway process is required.
Source discussion: agent-facing PLexus surfaces should be exactly two concepts: `pharo-launcher` for scoped image lifecycle and `gateway` for operating Pharo MCP tools inside selected images.

Goal: make the PLexus gateway surface the normal agent-facing Pharo MCP proxy and hide raw routing by default.

Acceptance criteria:
- Generated workspace MCP config exposes two normal agent-facing servers: `pharo-launcher` for scoped lifecycle and `gateway` for typed Pharo MCP calls routed by `imageId`.
- The current Pharo facade/proxy behavior is available through the `gateway` server name/surface: it lists project Pharo MCP tools, adds required `imageId`, validates the scoped route, strips `imageId`, and forwards to the selected image MCP server.
- `plexus_route_to_image` is not exposed in normal generated agent config and is not part of the default gateway tool list.
- Raw routing remains available only through an explicit opt-in setting such as `exposeRawRoutingTool` / environment or CLI flag; default is false.
- Route registration, unregister, status, and stale-route cleanup remain internal/admin plumbing for PLexus core or operators, not a third normal agent-facing surface.
- Existing compatibility names can remain temporarily if needed, but docs identify `gateway` as the agent-facing Pharo MCP proxy and mark old/default confusing surfaces for migration.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Do not launch live images, start a gateway service, mutate launcher state, or require external MCP servers.
- Coordinate with `plexus:local-8`; this item owns naming/default tool exposure, while `local-8` owns scoped project/workspace/image context and route metadata.

Verification:
- Add/update gateway server tests for default tool lists, opt-in raw routing exposure, and `gateway` surface behavior.
- Add/update workspace MCP config tests proving generated agent config contains `pharo-launcher` and `gateway`, and does not expose raw routing by default.
- Run PLexus typecheck/tests when feasible.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after verification passes.

items.title: Publish PLexus scoped dogfood npm packages
items.description:
Type: packaging/publication slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for metadata, dry-run packing, and registry publish when npm auth allows.
Source discussion: make PLexus lifecycle and Pharo MCP gateway packages available to more machines through npm without splitting the Git repository.

Goal: publish PLexus workspace packages as scoped npm dogfood prereleases while keeping the single PLexus repository and package boundary intact.

Acceptance criteria:
- Workspace packages use scoped Evref-BL npm names for lifecycle/core and gateway packages.
- Root PLexus remains the private monorepo aggregator; only workspace packages are published.
- Workspace package contents are constrained to dist/types/package metadata.
- npm pack dry-runs show expected package contents for both packages.
- npm publish uses public access and dogfood tag if npm auth allows; otherwise the blocker is recorded clearly.

Verification:
- npm run build.
- npm run typecheck --workspaces.
- npm test --workspaces.
- npm pack --dry-run for each workspace package.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after metadata and verification are complete.

local-1.body:
Completed in this cycle. Added docs/live-smoke-runner-boundary.md and linked it from docs/development.md. The boundary names disposable inputs, runner/process ownership, cleanup responsibilities, timeout behavior, retained artifacts, checks allowed without live services, checks requiring the runner, and approval criteria for future live PLexus smoke tests. No PLexus open/close, Docker, image launch, gateway live probe, package install, or host process mutation was performed.

Verification passed: git diff --check; npm run build; npm test. Published PLexus commit 916e1d5 to origin/main. Created blocked follow-up plexus:local-2 for the approved live-smoke runner execution.

local-2.body: Updated during dev-nexus:local-5 plan split to include explicit source-plan, blocker, verification, and publication notes. Live runtime execution remains blocked until the isolated runner is approved.

Completed by approved run `dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0428` after the launcher-side profile and detached-launch fixes landed.

Result:
- Copied `MCP12-2` to owned disposable image `PlexusSmokedev177898486735637520`.
- Opened PLexus project successfully; state after open recorded image `dev` as running on port 7101 with pid 29560.
- Verified image MCP tools/list returned 15 tools.
- Routed `find-packages` and received `7 packages found [scope=projects] (7 matched).`
- Closed project, verified process gone, route unregistered, closed target absent from gateway status, and deleted only the copied disposable image.
- Post-run launcher check found no remaining `Plexus*` disposable images or processes; only the existing Pharo Launcher UI process remained.

Artifacts: `C:\dev\code\dev-nexus-dogfood\.dev-nexus\runtime\artifacts\overnight-live-20260517\dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0428`.

Approved npm-path live smoke completed as dogfood-overnight-local-3-npm-20260517-0442. It copied MCP12-2 to an owned disposable image, opened PLexus, routed find-packages through the gateway, closed the project, verified route/process cleanup, and deleted the copied image. Artifacts are retained under .dev-nexus/runtime/artifacts/overnight-live-20260517/dogfood-overnight-local-3-npm-20260517-0442.

local-3.body: Still blocked on isolated runner approval. Smallest unblock is a named disposable runner profile: state root, image/template policy, allowed Docker or no-Docker mode, port range, timeout budget, artifact/log retention path, cleanup sequence, and failure policy. Until approved, only static/unit work should run.

Runner profile overnight-live-20260517 is approved and recorded in .dev-nexus/automation/runtime-profile-overnight-live-20260517.md. Docker/Podman works, local npm dependency repair is allowed in component/worktree scope, pharo-launcher-mcp is now under C:\\dev\\code\\git\\pharo-launcher-mcp, PLexus workspace package links were repaired, stale generated old-name package was pruned, and npm run smoke:open-route-close -- --help reaches the runner. Proceed with harness hardening or focused live runner validation inside that profile only.

Additional setup verification: pharo-launcher-mcp live-smoke passed using profile overnight-live-20260517 and StateRoot C:\\dev\\code\\dev-nexus-dogfood\\.dev-nexus\\runtime\\pharo-launcher\\overnight-live-20260517. It copied only the launcher image/changes into the disposable profile and passed health, version, and validate-installation checks. No source checkout changes were produced by the smoke.

Progress/blocker from overnight-live-harness-20260517:

Implemented a focused local PLexus harness hardening slice in `C:\dev\code\dev-nexus-dogfood\worktrees\plexus\local-3-live-smoke-harness-20260517`:
- `scripts/smoke-open-route-close.mjs` now requires an approval profile, isolated pharo-launcher-mcp profile root, disposable PLexus state root, artifact root, run id, workspace/target ids, and timeout budget before live execution.
- Added `scripts/live-smoke-runner-policy.mjs` with node-native policy tests and wired `npm test` to include them.
- Runner writes manifest/events and selected state/gateway artifacts under the approved artifact root, and records cleanup actions only for owned images/processes.

Verification passed:
- `node --test scripts/live-smoke-runner-policy.test.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/smoke-open-route-close.mjs`
- unapproved `node scripts/smoke-open-route-close.mjs --copyFromImageName MCP12-2` exits before launcher access with `--approvalProfile is required`
- `git diff --check`

Blocked live verification:
- Approved run `dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0152` used the recorded disposable PLexus state root, launcher profile root, artifact root, and `dogfood-overnight` workspace/target ids.
- The run failed before project open/route while copying `MCP12-2` to an owned `PlexusSmoke...` image: Pharo Launcher raised `PrimitiveFailed: primitive #createDirectory: in WindowsStore failed` from `PhLDirectoryBasedImageRepository>>prepareForNewImageNamed:`.
- Artifact evidence is retained at `C:\dev\code\dev-nexus-dogfood\.dev-nexus\runtime\artifacts\overnight-live-20260517\dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0152`.
- A retry with `PHARO_LAUNCHER_MCP_LAUNCHER_CONFIGURATION=overnight-live-20260517` failed earlier because Pharo Launcher looked for `C:\Users\gabriel.darbord\AppData\Local\Pharo Launcher\overnight-live-20260517`.

No PLexus project was opened, no route was registered, and the source `MCP12-2` image was not deleted or intentionally mutated. Next step is to fix or document the pharo-launcher-mcp isolated profile/config path used by image copy before re-running this PLexus smoke.

Dependency repair note from coordinator on 2026-05-17: the worker reported npm install could not reach @evref-bl/pharo-launcher-mcp and left partial node_modules. The package is currently reachable on npm at 0.1.1. I removed only this generated worktree's ignored node_modules after verifying the path stayed inside the local-3 worktree, then ran npm ci --ignore-scripts --no-audit --no-fund from the existing package-lock.

Focused verification now passes: npm ls @evref-bl/pharo-launcher-mcp --depth=0 resolves through local workspaces, and npm run test:smoke-policy passes 7 tests. Source edits in package.json and scripts/* were preserved and not changed by this repair. Durable DevNexus setup follow-up is recorded on dev-nexus:local-24.

Completed and published.

Commit: PLexus 5953274 (`Harden live smoke runner`) pushed to origin/main.

What changed:
- `scripts/smoke-open-route-close.mjs` now requires an approval profile, launcher profile root, artifact root, disposable state root, run/workspace/target identity, and timeout budget before live execution.
- Added `scripts/live-smoke-runner-policy.mjs` and policy tests for required inputs, disposable path boundaries, id prefix enforcement, timeout parsing, and copy-source/target alias rejection.
- The runner now records manifest/events plus before/open/close state and gateway artifacts, and cleanup records only owned process/image actions.

Verification:
- `npm run typecheck` passed.
- `npm test` passed: core 70 tests, gateway 28 tests, smoke policy 7 tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Approved live smoke `dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0428` passed copy, open, route `find-packages`, close, unregister, process stop, and copied-image delete. Artifacts retained at `C:\dev\code\dev-nexus-dogfood\.dev-nexus\runtime\artifacts\overnight-live-20260517\dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0428`.

Follow-up publication: PLexus now depends on @evref-bl/pharo-launcher-mcp@^0.1.2 in source commit 7d34f86. Verification passed with npm run typecheck, npm test, git diff --check, and approved live smoke dogfood-overnight-local-3-npm-20260517-0442 using the published package path instead of a local launcher checkout.

local-4.body: Promoted for parallel non-live dogfood wave. Scope: gateway lifecycle/package boundary split; no live gateway/image/process startup.

Completed and published to PLexus origin/main through a616dd4. Worker commit 0fe745a split gateway lifecycle ownership into core lifecycle/server surfaces and kept gateway routing-focused. Integrated verification passed: npm run typecheck --workspaces; npm test; node --check scripts/smoke-open-route-close.mjs; git diff --check.

local-7.body: Promoted for parallel non-live dogfood wave. Scope: OS-agnostic config tests/docs. Docker may be documented as an optional Linux verification path, but Docker must not be run in this wave without explicit isolated-runner approval.

Completed and published to PLexus origin/main through a616dd4. Worker commit e1afeea broadened OS-agnostic config/path tests and docs, including Docker as optional Linux verification documentation without running Docker. Integrated verification passed: npm run typecheck --workspaces; npm test; node --check scripts/smoke-open-route-close.mjs; git diff --check.

local-10.body: Completed. Published @evref-bl/plexus-core@0.1.0-alpha.0 and @evref-bl/plexus-gateway@0.1.0-alpha.0 to npm with dogfood tag and public access; source metadata/docs committed/pushed as PLexus ecac759. Verification passed: npm run build, npm run typecheck --workspaces, npm test --workspaces (core 70 tests, gateway 28 tests), npm pack --dry-run for both workspaces, npm publish for both workspaces, npm view bin metadata, npm exec --package @evref-bl/plexus-core@dogfood -- plexus --help, and tarball import smoke for @evref-bl/plexus-gateway.
